Module com.example.cincuentazo
Package com.example.cincuentazo.model.player

Class Player

java.lang.Object
com.example.cincuentazo.model.player.Player
public class Player extends Object
Represents a player in a game of Cincuentazo, either the human player or a machine opponent.

Each player holds a hand of cards. The hand is modeled with a Set because every card in a standard deck is unique, so duplicates are impossible by design. A LinkedHashSet is used to preserve the insertion order, which keeps the visual arrangement of the cards stable in the user interface.

A player can be marked as eliminated when they can no longer play any card without breaking the core rule of the game.

    Modifier and Type
    Field
    Description
    private boolean
    eliminated
    Whether this player has been eliminated from the game.
    private final Set<Card>
    hand
    The player's hand of cards (unique cards, insertion-ordered).
    static final int
    HAND_SIZE
    The maximum number of cards a player holds in hand.
    private final boolean
    machine
    Whether this player is controlled by the machine.
    private final String
    name
    The player's display name.

  • Constructor Details

    • Player

      public Player(String name, boolean machine)
      Creates a new player with the given name and type.
      Parameters:
      name - the player's display name; must not be null
      machine - true if controlled by the machine, false if it is the human player
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the name is null or blank

  • Method Details

    • addCard

      public void addCard(Card card)
      Adds a card to the player's hand.
      Parameters:
      card - the card to add; must not be null
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the card is null

    • removeCard

      public boolean removeCard(Card card)
      Removes a card from the player's hand.
      Parameters:
      card - the card to remove
      Returns:
      true if the card was in the hand and was removed

    • getHand

      public Set<Card> getHand()
      Returns an unmodifiable view of the player's hand, preventing external code from altering the hand directly.
      Returns:
      an unmodifiable set of the player's cards

    • getHandSize

      public int getHandSize()
      Returns the number of cards currently in the player's hand.
      Returns:
      the hand size

    • getName

      public String getName()
      Returns the player's display name.
      Returns:
      the name

    • isMachine

      public boolean isMachine()
      Indicates whether this player is controlled by the machine.
      Returns:
      true if it is a machine player

    • isEliminated

      public boolean isEliminated()
      Indicates whether this player has been eliminated.
      Returns:
      true if the player is eliminated

    • eliminate

      public void eliminate()
      Marks this player as eliminated.
